race.name: 3RIDES Gran Fondo Südliche Weinstrasse
race.tagline: UCI Gran Fondo qualifier through German vineyards and into France

vitals.location: Porte des Vins Allemands (German Wine Gate), Schweigen-Rechtenbach, Rhineland-Palatinate, Germany
vitals.location_badge: Porte des Vins Allemands (German Wine Gate), Germany

climate.description: April in Rhineland-Palatinate features mild spring weather suitable for outdoor cycling, though past events faced thunderstorm risks.
challenges: Precipitation: Moderate, with historical cancellations due to unstable weather

features: Rolling hills through Pfalz vineyards, forests, and a 15 km cross-border section into France

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 69. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 64.

course_description.character: Rolling 100 km loop: vineyards, forests, gentle hills; 15 km into France; technically challenging, car-free.
course_description.signature_challenge: 15 km French border section through wine trails

logistics.lodging_strategy: Local wineries, hotels like Land-gut-Hotel Schweigener Hof, and guesthouses abundant.
logistics.transport: Start/finish at landmark German Wine Gate near French border; previously in Aachen.

history.origin_story: Gran Fondo debut 2024 in Aachen; 2026 edition at Schweigen-Rechtenbach as 3RIDES Südliche Weinstrasse, evolved from 2023 gravel event.
notable_moments: Marianne Vos won 2024 women's race in Aachen.
Prior Gran Fondo canceled due to thunderstorms.

biased_opinion.summary: This UCI qualifier delivers a picturesque yet punchy ride through wine country, with the France border jaunt as a standout. Corrects user myths on distance/elevation; solid T2 road event for Euro spring training.

final_verdict.one_liner: Technically demanding UCI road qualifier in scenic Pfalz wine lands.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Relocated 100 km loop offers car-free challenge, cross-border adventure, and qualification path in a storied wine region; ideal for amateurs seeking prestige without gravel extremes.

citations.label: UCI Gran Fondo World Series Page
